---
title: "Why so many devices in 70-persistent-net.rules?"
date: 2014-02-10
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by vperaino on 2014-02-10
I recently learned how to rename devices as they appear when ifconfig is invoked (for my own compulsive reasons). 

But, why are there so many devices listed in this file? My laptop running Mint 13 only has two devices listed for the ethernet and wireless devices, but this Linux tower I have has all of these different devices populating this configuration file?

```
# This file was automatically generated by the /lib/udev/write_net_rules
# program, run by the persistent-net-generator.rules rules file.
#
# You can modify it, as long as you keep each rule on a single
# line, and change only the value of the NAME= key.

# PCI device 0x10ec:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:1c.0/0000:01:00.0 (r8169)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:1f:16:e8:a6:1e", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="eth*", NAME="eth3"

# PCI device 0x168c:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:1c.1/0000:02:00.0 (ath9k)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="0c:60:76:11:32:f6", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="wlan*", NAME="wlan8"

# PCI device 0x14e4:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:06.0/0000:02:00.0/ssb0:0 (b43-pci-bridge)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:24:2c:a2:3a:b6", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="wlan*", NAME="wlan1"

# PCI device 0x1969:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:07.0/0000:05:00.0 (atl1c)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:23:5a:db:42:0d", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="eth*", NAME="eth1"

# PCI device 0x14e4:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:06.0/0000:02:00.0/ssb0:0 (b43-pci-bridge)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:24:2c:a3:d7:4d", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="wlan*", NAME="wlan2"

# PCI device 0x14e4:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:06.0/0000:02:00.0/ssb0:0 (b43-pci-bridge)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:24:2c:a3:d7:4d", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="wlan*", NAME="wlan3"

# PCI device 0x168c:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:05.0/0000:02:00.0 (ath9k)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="78:e4:00:82:f2:eb", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="wlan*", NAME="wlan4"

# PCI device 0x168c:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:1e.0/0000:03:04.0 (ath5k)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:16:44:94:7c:ea", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="wlan*", NAME="wlan5"

# PCI device 0x8086:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:1c.1/0000:02:00.0 (e1000e)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:01:80:67:5a:48", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="eth*", NAME="eth2"

# PCI device 0x168c:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:1e.0/0000:03:04.0 (ath5k)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:c0:a8:ff:a3:ae", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="wlan*", NAME="wlan6"

# PCI device 0x14e4:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:04.0/0000:02:00.0/ssb0:0 (b43-pci-bridge)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="90:4c:e5:ac:9e:87", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="wlan*", NAME="wlan7"

# PCI device 0x11ab:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:1e.0/0000:01:04.0 (skge)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:1a:92:95:54:1c", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="eth*", NAME="eth0"

# PCI device 0x168c:/sys/devices/pci0000:00/0000:00:1e.0/0000:01:00.0 (ath5k)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="00:14:6c:8c:98:b7", ATTR{dev_id}=="0x0", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="wlan*", NAME="wlan0"
```

---

### Post by praseodym on 2014-02-10
Set up a new one:```


sudo mv /etc/udev/rules.d/70-persistent-net.rules /etc/udev/rules.d/70-persistent-net.bak
sudo udevadm trigger
sudo service udev reload
sudo service udev restart
```

---

### Post by vperaino on 2014-02-10
Nifty!

Thanks a bunch. :)

---
